title: Response to egfr blockade
abstract: Recent large-scale analyses have demonstrated that the genomic landscape of human cancer is complex and variable among individuals of the same tumor type. Such underlying genetic differences may in part be responsible for the varying therapeutic responses observed in cancer patients. To examine the effect of somatic genetic changes in colorectal cancer on sensitivity to a common targeted therapy, we performed complete exome sequence and copy number analyses of 129 tumors that were KRAS wild-type and analyzed their response to anti-EGFR antibody blockade in patient-derived tumorgraft models. In addition to previously identified genes, we detected mutations in ERBB2, EGFR, FGFR1, PDGFRA, and MAP2K1 as potential mechanisms of primary resistance to this therapy. Alterations in the ectodomain of EGFR were identified in patients with acquired resistance to EGFR blockade. Amplifications and sequence changes in the tyrosine kinase receptor adaptor gene IRS2 were identified in tumors with increased sensitivity to anti-EGFR therapy. Therapeutic resistance to EGFR blockade could be overcome in tumorgraft models through combinatorial therapies targeting actionable genes. These analyses provide a systematic approach to evaluate response to targeted therapies in human cancer, highlight additional mechanisms of responsiveness to anti-EGFR therapies, and provide additional avenues for intervention in the management of colorectal cancer.
claims: Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A method of treating a tumor in a human, comprising: testing a sample from the tumor and determining an amplification or an activating mutation in tyrosine kinase receptor adaptor gene IRS2; and treating or prescribing to the human an inhibitor of a receptor selected from the group consisting of: MET, ERBB2, EGFR, FGFR, and PDGFR. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the inhibitor is an anti-EGFR antibody. 3. The method of claim 1 wherein the inhibitor is cetuximab. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein the sample is further tested to determine absence of a BRAF mutation and absence of MET amplification. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein the tumor is a colorectal tumor. 6. A method of treating a tumor in a human, wherein the tumor is resistant to EGFR blockade, comprising: administering to the human an inhibitor of FGFR1 and an inhibitor of EGFR. 7. The method of claim 6 wherein the inhibitor of FGFR1 is an FGFR1 kinase inhibitor. 8. The method of claim 6 wherein the EGFR inhibitor is an anti-EGFR antibody. 9. The method of claim 6 wherein the FGFR1 inhibitor is BGJ398. 10. The method of claim 6 wherein the EGFR inhibitor is cetuximab. 11. The method of claim 6 wherein FGFR1 is amplified in the tumor. 12. The method of claim 6 wherein prior to the step of administering, a sample from the tumor is tested and amplification of FGFR1 is determined. 13. The method of claim 6 wherein the tumor is a colorectal tumor. 14. A method of treating a tumor in a human, wherein the tumor is resistant to EGFR blockade, comprising: administering an EGFR kinase inhibitor and an anti-EGFR antibody to the human. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein the anti-EGFR antibody is cetuximab. 16. The method of claim 14 wherein the EFGR kinase inhibitor is afatinib. 17. The method of claim 14 wherein prior to the step of administering, a sample from the tumor is tested and presence of a mutation in EGFR is found in a minority of tumor cells in the sample. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the mutation is V843I. 19. The method of claim 17 wherein the mutation is G465E. 20. The method of claim 17 wherein the mutation is G465R. 21. The method of claim 14 wherein the tumor is a colorectal tumor. 22. A method of treating a tumor in a human, wherein the tumor is resistant to EGFR blockade, comprising: administering to the human an inhibitor of MEK1 and an inhibitor of ERK. 23. The method of claim 22 wherein the inhibitor of MEK1 is AZD6244. 24. The method of claim 22 wherein the inhibitor of ERK is SCH772984. 25. The method of claim 22 wherein prior to the step of administering, a sample from the tumor is tested and presence of a mutation in MAP2K1 is identified in the sample. 26. The method of claim 25 wherein the mutation is a K57N mutation. 27. The method of claim 22 wherein the tumor is a colorectal tumor. 28. A method of treating a tumor in a human, wherein the tumor is resistant to EGFR blockade, comprising: administering to the human a monoclonal antibody to EGFR that binds to an epitope distinct from the epitopes bound by cetuximab and panitimumab. 29. The method of claim 28 wherein the monoclonal antibody is supplied in a mixture of monoclonal antibodies to several ERBB family members. 30. The method of claim 28 wherein prior to the step of administering, a sample from the tumor is tested and presence of a mutation in EGFR is determined. 31. The method of claim 30 wherein the mutation is G465E. 32. The method of claim 30 wherein the mutation is G465R. 33. The method of claim 28 wherein the tumor is a colorectal tumor. 34. A method of treating a human with a tumor, comprising: testing a sample from the tumor and determining a mutation in a gene selected from the group consisting of: FGFR, PDGFRÄ®, MAP2K1, and ERBB2; treating the human with an antibody to EGFR. 35. A method of treating a human with a tumor, comprising: treating a tumor with a first antibody to EGFR; testing a sample from the tumor and determining a mutation in EGFR!s ectodomain; modifying the treatment to include an EGFR kinase inhibitor or an anti-EGFR antibody to a distinct epitope from the epitope bound by the first antibody. 36. The method of claim 35 wherein the mutation is in codon 465.
